---
title: "SM ES2"
author: "Juliette Limozin"
date: "2023-11-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(MASS)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
?hills
pairs(hills)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(hills$time ~ hills$dist)
plot(hills$time ~ hills$climb)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

```{r}
hills[hills$time > 50 & hills$dist <5,]
hills[hills$time > 180 & hills$dist > 15,]
```

```{r}
hills['Knock Hill', 'time'] <- hills['Knock Hill', 'time'] - 60
```


```{r}
plot(log(hills$time) ~ log(hills$dist))
plot(log(hills$time) ~ log(hills$climb))
```


```{r}
lm1 <- lm(log(time) ~ log(dist), data = hills)
summary(lm1)
plot(lm1)
```
```{r}
lm2 <- lm(log(time) ~ log(dist) + log(climb), data = hills)
summary(lm2)
plot(lm2)
```

```{r}
anova(lm1, lm2)
```

```{r}
predict.lm(lm2, data.frame(dist = 5.3,climb = 1100), interval = 'prediction', level = 0.95)
predict.lm(lm1, data.frame(dist = 5.3,climb = 1100), interval = 'prediction', level = 0.95)
```

```{r}
paste0('Model with climb: ',exp(3.556545),' (', exp(3.224142), ', ',exp(3.888948), ')' )
paste0('Model without climb: ',exp(3.561158),' (', exp(3.11599), ', ',exp(4.006326), ')' )
```
